title: Friday Sermon 17 February 1984
description:
God has given glad tidings of His special help and succour for the Ahmadiyya Jamaat in three of my recent dreams. Even if there are some adverse developments, Allah will protect the Community with
His special Grace. These glad tidings put some responsibilities on us, namely:

I.  The Jamaat should strive to prove worthy of this special Divine Help.

II.  The Jamaat must absorb itself in the task of calling towards Allah with such eagerness, zeal and fervent prayers that Allah favours us with sweet fruits.

One should understand the philosophy of prayer. The Promised Messiah has given some recipes for prayers which meet with a special acceptance from God.

There are three things without which prayers do not attain strength and fervency:

I.  Prayers gain strength from sadness.

II. Prayers gain strength from the happiness which converts into a sense of deep gratitude towards God. 

III.  Prayers get strength from love. Deep love requires that the lover be deeply hurt to hear anything against the beloved. It saddens him exceedingly and a prayer out of the wounded heart cannot go unanswered.

I advise Ahmadis to become true votaries of Allah. Remember Allah always and under all conditions.  When God becomes your Protector, no power on earth can make you perish. It is very important that we become thankful creatures of God and go out in the service of the faith like madmen. When we become embodiments of the Love of God, we will witness His marvels. Getting our strength from the Grace of God, with our untiring efforts and our fervent prayers, we will bring about the revolution for Islam for our age which was foretold by the Holy Prophet, peace and blessings of God be on him.
